refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: analyzing terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and clarity, it is necessary to separate these into distinct sub-FRs, each focusing on a single aspect of worksite analysis. This refinement isolates the terrain analysis as one atomic functional requirement.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify by demonstrating that the system can generate and provide detailed terrain data of the worksite independently of other features.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain.
The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of obstacles within the worksite area.
The NGHE solution shall provide representation of the work-zone within the worksite area.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing key site features
Extract and classify terrain features relevant to safety and operations
Create a geospatial map integrating terrain data with safety and policy overlays
Provide an interface for planning and execution modules to query terrain information
selected_dp.selected_dp: Generate a digital terrain model representing key site features

potential_sources: Integration of terrain model with planning and execution modules
coupling_check.notes: Selecting a digital terrain model as the DP allows independent development of the model and its use by other functions, supporting modularity and minimizing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate raw terrain data for model generation
Risk that terrain model may not capture all features required by safety and operational policies
Assumes planning and execution functions can consume the terrain model format without tight integration
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining interfaces and data formats between the terrain model and planning/execution functions to ensure loose coupling and ease of integration.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: This module encompasses the core processing and data management functions of the system, including terrain modeling and site feature analysis.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives sensor and positional data necessary for terrain model generation.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands and parameters for generating the digital terrain model.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Sends processed terrain model data to other system components or external users.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as terrain modeling is a core processing function; minimal diffusion since data inputs and outputs are well encapsulated.
Low containment as vehicle focuses on mobility and control; terrain modeling would require extensive data sharing and cause high diffusion.
Very low containment; teleoperation is for remote control, not data processing; would cause significant diffusion.
Minimal relevance; safety services focus on alerts and monitoring, not terrain modeling; high diffusion expected.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing the digital terrain model generation DP because it naturally contains the data processing and modeling functions with minimal diffusion outside the module.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on real-time telemetry data availability may cause interface complexity.
If terrain model data is needed by multiple modules, ensuring efficient data sharing without duplication is necessary.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Generate a digital terrain model representing key site features' directly satisfies the FR 'The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain' by producing a digital representation of the terrain, which is the core analytical output required.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on real-time telemetry data availability may cause interface complexity.
If terrain model data is needed by multiple modules, ensuring efficient data sharing without duplication is necessary.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating that the DP is well encapsulated within a single module responsible for terrain modeling and site feature analysis. Other modules have low containment and high diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module receives telemetry and tasking inputs and outputs processed terrain data via defined interfaces, enabling testing against acceptance criteria for model generation and availability to planning/execution functions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership candidate 'NGHE Solution' clearly aligns with the DP and FR, with well-defined scope and interfaces, minimizing ambiguity in responsibility allocation.

recommended_revisions: Clarify interface protocols and data formats for telemetry input to reduce complexity and ensure reliable real-time data availability.
Define data sharing mechanisms or APIs for terrain model data distribution to other modules to prevent duplication and manage diffusion risk.
Document contingency plans for telemetry data unavailability to maintain terrain model generation capability or degrade gracefully.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current functional requirement is adequately satisfied by the design parameter and properly encapsulated within the implementation module. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed, and containment and diffusion risks are low and manageable. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to stop decomposition here to maintain clarity and control over the critical function. Recommended revisions focus on interface and data sharing clarifications rather than further decomposition.

revise.why: Clarify interface protocols and data formats for telemetry input, define data sharing mechanisms for terrain model data, and document contingency plans to reduce complexity and manage diffusion risk.

assumptions: The DP fully satisfies the FR as per gate evaluation.
The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate implementation boundary with high containment and low diffusion.
Verification and allocation are feasible and clear.
open_risks: Dependency on real-time telemetry data availability may cause interface complexity.
Potential data duplication if terrain model data is shared inefficiently across modules.
evidence_needed: Detailed interface protocol and data format specifications for telemetry input.
Defined APIs or mechanisms for terrain model data sharing.
Contingency plans for telemetry data unavailability.
